content:
4 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

That is how long our internal dashboard took to load on the office WiFi, and that is how long it took for three different teams to stop trusting the data on their screens.
People had started refreshing the page twice out of habit before they believed the numbers in front of them. Nobody complained directly. They just quietly stopped using the tool, and started asking for the same numbers over email instead.
That silence is what actually got my attention, not a Lighthouse score, not a ticket, just a slow drop in usage that nobody could explain until I went looking.
If you have ever opened your bundle analyzer, seen a number that made your stomach drop, and closed the tab pretending you did not see it, this one is for you.
The Setup Nobody Asks For Until It Is Too Late
The dashboard tracked inventory across twelve regions. Fifty components in total, built in React, state handled through a mix of Redux and habit more than design.
Every click triggered a re-render somewhere it had no business touching. Every new feature pulled in another package, and node_modules had quietly grown heavier than the actual product it supported.
